summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a high‑energy 1987 show with standout Jack Straw and a smoking Devil > Good Golly > Devil medley.
review: The performance captures the raw, upbeat vibe of late‑summer ’87, highlighted by a powerful opening Jack Straw and a fiery Devil > Good Golly > Devil sequence, plus strong vocal moments on Not Fade Away. The soundboard source is crystal clear – reviewers call it one of the best‑sounding releases of this era – though a few songs (e.g., Eyes, Drums) feel flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both the musical energy and the pristine soundboard quality; only one review is mixed, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with no reported drop‑outs or distortion; reviewers note it may be the best‑sounding version of this concert.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong, punchy first set opens with Jack Straw and stays energetic; the second set is uneven – some moments (Hell, Estimated Prophet) are solid while others feel detached, but overall the show maintains a high‑octane 1987 vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocal enthusiasm (especially on Push/NFA) and lead‑guitar tone during Miracle receive frequent praise.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s playing is noted as mixed – praised for the Blue Dress > Good Golly medley but called ‘obliviously silly’ in places.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum break is described as unmemorable.
